title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false
input_row.description: Disable anonymous requests to the Kubelet server.
input_row.rationale: When enabled, requests that are not rejected by other configured authentication methods are treated as anonymous requests. These requests are then served by the Kubelet server. You should rely on authentication to authorize access and disallow anonymous requests.
input_row.audit: In OpenShift 4, the Kubernetes configuration file is managed by the Machine Config Operator and anonymous-auth is set to false by default. Run the following command on each node to the configuration of anonymous authentication: for node in $(oc get nodes -ojsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'); do oc get --raw /api/v1/nodes/$node/proxy/configz | jq '.kubeletconfig.authentication.anonymous.enabled' done Verify that the configuration for each node returns false .
input_row.remediation: Create a kubeletconfig to explicitly disable anonymous authentication. Examples of how to do this can be found in the OpenShift documentation .
input_row.default_value: By default, anonymous access is set to false .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
- The control can be fully verified via IBM Cloud APIs/IBM Cloud CLI/IBM Cloud CLI. The audit procedure detailed in the audit field is specific, leveraging the IBM Cloud CLI command `oc get --raw /api/v1/nodes/$node/proxy/configz`. Moreover, the reference to Kubernetes architecture and configurations indicates that this control directly relates to Kubernetes cluster management, which is well-supported within IBM Cloud APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_cluster_anonymous_auth_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Utilize the OpenShift API through IBM Cloud CLI commands to verify if anonymous authentication is disabled on all nodes within the Kubernetes cluster.
- The specific command to execute: 
  ```
  for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'); do
    oc get --raw /api/v1/nodes/$node/proxy/configz | jq '.kubeletconfig.authentication.anonymous.enabled'
  done
  ```
- This command retrieves the Kubernetes configuration for each node and checks the property related to anonymous authentication.
- The specific resource to enumerate: all nodes in the OpenShift cluster.
- The configuration property to validate: `.kubeletconfig.authentication.anonymous.enabled` should return `false`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** If all nodes return `false`, the audit is considered compliant (Pass); if any node returns `true`, it is non-compliant (Fail). 
- This process ensures checking against the requirement to disable anonymous requests to the Kubelet server, facilitating an essential security posture measurement.
